content.problemStatement: You are given two integers NNN and KKK. You may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero): change NNN to N‚àíKN-KN‚àíK, i.e. subtract KKK from NNN. Find the smallest non-negative integer value of NNN you can obtain this way.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the smallest value you can get.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

First, we change N=5N = 5N=5 to N‚àíK=5‚àí2=3N - K = 5 - 2 = 3N‚àíK=5‚àí2=3.
Then, we have N=3N = 3N=3 and we change it to N‚àíK=3‚àí2=1N - K = 3 - 2 = 1N‚àíK=3‚àí2=1.

Since 1<K1 \lt K1<K, the process stops here and the smallest value is 111.
Example case 2: We change N=4N = 4N=4 to N‚àíK=4‚àí4=0N - K = 4 - 4 = 0N‚àíK=4‚àí4=0. Since 0<K0 \lt K0<K, the process stops here and the smallest value is 000.
Example case 3: Since 2<K2 \lt K2<K initially, we should not perform any operations and the smallest value is 222.
